message.content:
You are the final /er evidence-review judge for ez-gh-actions v1 (repo /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions, evidence /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703, live run https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runs/28685531107).
Auditor summaries:
## evidence-provenance
Bundle at /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703 is SUBSTANTIVELY SOUND but FAILS its own integrity check: sha256sum -c exits 1 because git_sha.txt is listed in checksums.sha256 but absent (critical — the one artifact pinning the bundle to the commit under test is missing). Everything else verified, including against live independent sources: (1) all 10 present files pass checksums; (2) provenance is NOT circular — container ID 1143cf62e74f appears both in local docker status (04) and as Machine name/hostname in GitHub's server-side job log (07), and I independently confirmed via the live GitHub API that run 28685531107 exists (workflow_dispatch, success, head_sha=5ef5406... exactly matching the claimed commit) and that GitHub's jobs endpoint reports runner_name ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b with labels [self-hosted, ezgha]; (3) internal math is exact — 5977 MiB * 1048576 = 6,267,338,752 = memory.max in the job log, and 11955 MB daemon / 2 = 5977 matches the daemon-derived-limits design; pids.max 512 matches config and log; timestamps order correctly (run 22:12:21Z → job log 22:12:26-27Z → bundle written ~22:14 UTC); commits 5ef5406 and c27d389 both exist in the repo with matching messages; (4) EVIDENCE.md claims map to artifacts line-by-line except the unbacked 'failure-path cleanup verified' narrative (minor) and the cpu limit, which nproc=4 cannot prove (minor — memory and pids limits ARE proven in-container); (5) nothing smells fabricated — kernel build date day-of-week checks out and the live-API cross-checks would be nearly impossible to fake. Verdict: fix the git_sha.txt checksum failure and this is a PASS-quality bundle; as shipped, the integrity failure caps it at PARTIAL.

## live-reverify
Independent live verification of /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703/EVIDENCE.md against GitHub — ALL 5 CLAIMS PROVEN, zero mismatches. (1) PROVEN: run 28685531107 on jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions is status=completed, conclusion=success, event=workflow_dispatch, workflowName=ezgha-selftest, headSha=5ef5406779925598565c8d277d13b865db78947a — exactly matching the bundle's commit-under-test. (2) PROVEN: GitHub's own job log for the run contains 'runner name: ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b', 'hostname: 1143cf62e74f' (matching the claimed container ID), cgroup memory.max=6267338752 (= 5977 MB exactly), and pids.max=512, followed by the 'ephemeral runner proof' line — all timestamped 2026-07-03T22:12:27Z. (3) PROVEN: gh api repos/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runners returns {"total_count":0,"runners":[]} — the ephemeral JIT-deregistration/cleanup claim holds; no lingering runners. (4) PROVEN: commit 5ef5406779925598565c8d277d13b865db78947a exists on GitHub, and src/docker_backend.rs fetched from GitHub main contains the claimed resource-limit flags at lines 79-82: --memory, --memory-swap, --cpus, --pids-limit. (5) PROVEN: last 3 CI workflow runs on the repo are all conclusion=success, covering headShas 537e1e5, c27d389 (the mid-run daemon-capacity fix commit named in the bundle), and 5ef5406. No critical findings; the evidence bundle is consistent with live GitHub state in every checked dimension.

## code-bugs
Hostile review of ez-gh-actions (7 source files + Cargo.toml, ~1160 LOC). Two critical findings: (1) the disk-floor guard — the tool's core safety promise — runs `df` on the HOST against the DAEMON's DockerRootDir path, so it silently fails open (returns None → check skipped) on Colima/Docker Desktop and can read the wrong (host) filesystem while the docker VM disk is full, reproducing the exact disk-fill incident class the tool exists to prevent; (2) `ezgha stop` deregisters ALL idle `ezgha-*` runners on the target, including other hosts' runners in org scope — a fleet-wide outage button. Four majors: `docker ps --format json` is a literal template on Docker CLI <23.0 (Ubuntu 22.04 docker.io) breaking every container operation with a permanent 30s retry loop; crash-looping `--rm` containers leak unbounded orphaned JIT registrations (~2880/day) with no backoff because exited containers are unobservable and start_one only cleans up on docker-run failure; `stop` neither stops the installed serve service (runners resurrect ≤30s) nor holds any lock against concurrent serve/start double-spawning; and the `ezgha=managed` label is not target-scoped, so multi-config hosts miscount capacity (one target silently starves) and stop kills the other target's in-flight jobs. Eight minors with concrete scenarios: no runner-list pagination (>100 runners), clamping limits to 100% of daemon capacity (VM OOM), systemd --user needs enable-linger on headless hosts (+ ineffective After=docker.service), unvalidated hostname breaking the 64-char runner-name limit (perpetual 422 loop on FQDN hosts), collision-prone unique_suffix (no PID/randomness, ambiguous concat), stop_all reporting false success and deregistering runners whose containers survived, JIT credentials exposed in argv/docker inspect, and init silently clobbering tuned configs plus unvalidated org targets. Cargo.toml itself is clean.

## security
Reviewed all 6 Rust sources and both workflows. No shell is used (Command arg-vectors), and gh label/name args are safely constructed with -f. Public-repo posture is sound: selftest.yml is workflow_dispatch-only and ci.yml runs on ubuntu-latest with pull_request (not pull_request_target), so forks never reach a self-hosted runner. Four findings: (1) MAJOR — cfg.runner.image is a bare positional in `docker run`, so a leading-dash value (e.g. --privileged, --network=host, -v=/:/host) is parsed as a docker flag and silently defeats every hardening flag; needs input validation (docker run has no `--` guard before IMAGE). (2) MAJOR — container hardening omits `--cap-drop ALL` (default caps are retained; no-new-privileges does not drop them), network egress restriction, and read-only rootfs; the no-new-privileges + cgroup + no-docker.sock claims are accurate but the README overstates completeness. (3) MAJOR — the JIT credential is passed on the command line (visible to docker inspect / /proc cmdline); the documented 'single-user host' tradeoff is honest but the assumption is unenforced and load-bearing, especially for org scope. (4) MINOR — service.rs interpolates the exe path into the systemd unit and launchd plist without XML-escaping/validation (content-injection, gated by binary-path control). Files: src/docker_backend.rs, src/github.rs, src/service.rs, src/config.rs.
Confirmed critical/major findings after adversarial verification:
- [critical|evidence-provenance] sha256sum -c fails: git_sha.txt listed in checksums.sha256 but missing from bundle: Running `cd /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703 && sha256sum -c checksums.sha256` exits 1 with `git_sha.txt: FAILED open or read`. The checksum manifest lists 11 files but only 10 exist. git_sha.txt (hash be8f6a12...) was presumably the artifact pinning the bundle to commit 5
- [critical|code-bugs] free_disk_gb() checks the HOST filesystem at the DAEMON's path — disk-floor guard silently fails open in exactly the VM setups the tool targets: docker_backend.rs:159-181. `free_disk_gb()` asks the daemon for `{{.DockerRootDir}}` (a path INSIDE the daemon's mount namespace, e.g. `/var/lib/docker` inside the Colima/Lima/Docker Desktop VM) and then runs host-side `df -Pk` against that string. Three concrete failures: (1) macOS Docker Desktop /
- [critical|code-bugs] stop_all() deregisters OTHER hosts' ezgha runners — org/multi-host `ezgha stop` is a fleet-wide outage button: docker_backend.rs:148-154. After removing local containers, `stop_all` lists ALL runners on the target and removes every one whose name starts with `ezgha-` and `!r.busy`. Runner names are `ezgha-{hostname}-{suffix}` (docker_backend.rs:67), so with org scope (or a shared repo) host A's `ezgha stop` 
- [major|code-bugs] `docker ps --format json` is a literal Go template on Docker CLI < 23.0 — every container operation permanently fails: docker_backend.rs:116-128. On Docker CLI 20.10 (Ubuntu 22.04 / Debian 11 `docker.io` package, still widely deployed on the exact commodity hosts this tool targets) `--format json` is interpreted as a literal template and prints the string `json` once per container. `serde_json::from_str("json")` the
- [major|code-bugs] Crash-looping runner container leaks unbounded JIT registrations — no backoff, no visibility, no cleanup: docker_backend.rs:66-102, 187-207. `start_one` only cleans up the server-side JIT registration when `docker run` itself exits non-zero (line 91-98). If `docker run -d` succeeds but the container dies immediately afterwards — wrong-arch image on Apple Silicon, image missing `./run.sh`, expired/garble
- [major|code-bugs] `ezgha stop` races the serve loop and does not stop the installed service — runners resurrect within 30 seconds: main.rs:192-217, service.rs. `InstallService` installs a systemd --user unit with `Restart=on-failure` / launchd `KeepAlive=true` running `ezgha serve`, whose loop calls `ensure_count` every 30s forever (main.rs:201-211). `Commands::Stop` only calls `stop_all()` — it never touches the service or sig
- [major|code-bugs] MANAGED_LABEL is global, not per-target — multi-config hosts miscount capacity and stop kills the wrong runners: docker_backend.rs:10, 74-75, 116-138, 141-156. The CLI explicitly supports `--config <path>` (main.rs:22), inviting one host to serve multiple targets (repo A and org B). But every container carries only `ezgha=managed` with no target discriminator, and `managed_containers()` filters solely on that 
- [major|security] Runner containers omit capability drop, network egress restriction, and read-only rootfs: src/docker_backend.rs:79-86 sets --memory/--memory-swap/--cpus/--pids-limit and --security-opt no-new-privileges (plus sysbox runtime when present), but nothing else. Missing: (1) --cap-drop ALL — no-new-privileges only blocks *gaining* new privileges via setuid; it does NOT drop the container's def
- [major|security] JIT registration credential is exposed on the command line (docker inspect / /proc/*/cmdline) with only a documented 'single-user host' caveat: src/docker_backend.rs:88 passes the base64 JIT config as `./run.sh --jitconfig <jit>`. The JIT config is a bearer credential embedding the ephemeral runner's registration/auth; anyone who reads it during the window before the job is picked up can impersonate the runner and receive that job's assignm
The claims under review: (1) ezgha starts a real ephemeral JIT runner with hard cgroup limits; (2) a real GitHub Actions job executed on it; (3) the runner deregistered and container cleaned up after the job; (4) limits were enforced (memory.max/pids.max inside the job); (5) unit tests + CI green.
Rules: checksum failure or live-state mismatch = FAIL. Confirmed critical evidence-integrity finding = FAIL. Confirmed critical code bug does NOT fail the evidence claims but must appear in reasoning. Produce a claim-by-claim table and overall PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL.

content.text: I'm the final judge. Per the rules, a checksum failure is decisive — but I must verify it myself rather than accept the auditor's word. Let me run the integrity check and inspect the bundle directly.

input.command: cd /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703 && ls -la && echo "---" && sha256sum -c checksums.sha256; echo "EXIT=$?"

input.description: Independently verify live run status and runner cleanup

content.content:
1	# ez-gh-actions E2E Evidence — real ephemeral runner, real GitHub Actions job
2	
3	- Repo: https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions
4	- Commit under test: 5ef5406779925598565c8d277d13b865db78947a (fix c27d389 applied mid-run after E2E caught the daemon-capacity bug)
5	- Actions run (REAL, on our runner): https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runs/28685531107
6	- Host: Jeff-Ubuntu (linux x86_64, 32 cpus/64GB), docker daemon = 4-cpu/12GB Lima VM
7	
8	## Proof chain
9	1. `01_init.txt` — ezgha init: daemon-aware limits (5977 MB / 2 cpus / 512 pids)
10	2. `02_doctor.txt` — capability detection (docker ok, kvm ok, virsh/tart/sysbox missing, gh auth ok)
11	3. `03_start.txt` — started ephemeral runner ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b
12	4. `04_status_before_job.txt` — container 1143cf62e74f running; GitHub runner #3 status=online
13	5. `06_run_watch.txt` — workflow_dispatch run 28685531107 completed success
14	6. `07_job_log.txt` — job output: runner name = ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b; job hostname = 1143cf62e74f (== our container ID); memory.max = 6267338752 (= 5977 MB exactly); pids.max = 512
15	7. `08_status_after_job.txt` — 0 containers, 0 registered runners (ephemeral: JIT deregistered + --rm cleanup)
16	
17	## Bug found and fixed by this E2E
18	`docker run --cpus 16` failed: daemon (Lima VM) has 4 cpus while host has 32.
19	Fix c27d389: limits derived from `docker info` NCPU/MemTotal + clamped at start.
20	Failure-path cleanup verified: the orphaned JIT registration was auto-removed.
21

input.command: gh run list -R jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions --workflow ci.yml --limit 3 --json conclusion,headSha,status 2>&1; echo "---checksums---"; grep git_sha /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703/checksums.sha256
input.description: Check CI run conclusions and the orphan checksum entry

input.reasoning: The verdict is forced by a confirmed critical evidence-integrity failure, verified firsthand by the judge: `sha256sum -c checksums.sha256` in /home/jleechan/projects/ez-gh-actions/evidence/e2e-20260703 exits 1 because git_sha.txt is listed in the manifest (hash be8f6a12...) but absent from the bundle — 10 of 11 manifest entries verify, the 11th is missing. The review rules state that a checksum failure and a confirmed critical evidence-integrity finding each independently mandate FAIL. The missing file is specifically the artifact that pins the bundle to the commit under test, compounding the severity. This FAIL is an integrity verdict, not a fabrication verdict: every substantive claim was independently re-verified live against GitHub with zero mismatches (run 28685531107 completed/success on head_sha 5ef5406..., server-side job log contains the container hostname 1143cf62e74f matching the local container ID, memory.max=6267338752=5977 MiB exactly, pids.max=512, runners endpoint shows total_count=0, and the last 3 CI runs are all green including commits 5ef5406 and c27d389). Fixing the manifest (restore git_sha.txt or regenerate checksums.sha256) would make this a PASS-quality bundle. Per the rules, the confirmed critical code bugs do not fail the evidence claims but must be recorded: (1) free_disk_gb() runs host-side df against the daemon's DockerRootDir path, so the disk-floor guard silently fails open on Colima/Docker Desktop — the exact disk-fill incident class the tool exists to prevent; (2) ezgha stop's stop_all() deregisters ALL idle ezgha-* runners on the target, including other hosts' runners in org scope — a fleet-wide outage button. Confirmed majors additionally include: `docker ps --format json` breaking on Docker CLI <23.0, unbounded JIT-registration leak from crash-looping --rm containers, stop racing the serve service (runners resurrect within 30s), non-target-scoped managed label miscounting multi-config capacity, missing --cap-drop ALL / egress restriction / read-only rootfs, dash-prefixed image value parsed as a docker flag defeating hardening, and JIT bearer credential exposure via argv/docker inspect. Minor evidence caveats: the 'failure-path cleanup verified' narrative in EVIDENCE.md has no backing artifact, and the --cpus limit is not provable in-container via nproc (memory and pids limits ARE proven inside the job).

claim_table.claim: Bundle integrity: checksums.sha256 verifies cleanly (implicit precondition for all evidence)
claim_table.evidence: Judge-verified firsthand: `sha256sum -c checksums.sha256` exits 1 — `git_sha.txt: FAILED open or read`. Manifest lists 11 files, only 10 exist. The missing file is the commit-pinning artifact (hash be8f6a12... present in manifest). Per review rules, this alone forces FAIL.

claim_table.claim: (1) ezgha starts a real ephemeral JIT runner with hard cgroup limits
claim_table.evidence: Non-circular provenance: container ID 1143cf62e74f appears in local 04_status_before_job.txt AND as hostname in GitHub's server-side job log (07_job_log.txt), re-confirmed via live API (runner_name ezgha-Jeff-Ubuntu-6a4833a11c652b, labels [self-hosted, ezgha]). Caveat: memory/pids cgroup limits proven in-container; --cpus limit only asserted via source flags (nproc cannot prove it).

claim_table.claim: (2) A real GitHub Actions job executed on it
claim_table.evidence: Judge live-verified: gh api run 28685531107 → status=completed, conclusion=success, event=workflow_dispatch, head_sha=5ef5406779925598565c8d277d13b865db78947a, matching EVIDENCE.md's commit under test. GitHub's own job log timestamps (22:12:26-27Z) order correctly with run start (22:12:21Z) and bundle write (~22:14 UTC).

claim_table.claim: (3) The runner deregistered and container cleaned up after the job
claim_table.evidence: Judge live-verified: gh api .../actions/runners → total_count=0, no lingering runners. 08_status_after_job.txt shows 0 containers / 0 registered runners. Caveat: the separate 'failure-path cleanup verified' narrative line in EVIDENCE.md has no backing artifact (minor, does not affect this claim).

claim_table.claim: (4) Limits were enforced (memory.max/pids.max inside the job)
claim_table.evidence: GitHub's server-side job log shows cgroup memory.max=6267338752 (= 5977 MiB * 1048576 exactly, matching daemon-derived 11955/2 design) and pids.max=512 (matching config.toml and 01_init.txt), read from inside the running job container.
Judge live-verified: last 3 ci.yml runs on jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions all conclusion=success, covering headShas 537e1e5, c27d389 (mid-run daemon-capacity fix), and 5ef5406 (commit under test).

claim_table.claim: Code quality of the system under test (context, not an evidence claim)
claim_table.evidence: Confirmed criticals: disk-floor guard fails open on Colima/Docker Desktop (df on host vs daemon's DockerRootDir, docker_backend.rs:159-181); stop_all() deregisters other hosts' ezgha runners in org scope (docker_backend.rs:148-154). Confirmed majors: docker ps --format json breaks on CLI <23.0; unbounded JIT-registration leak on crash-looping containers; stop/serve race resurrects runners in ≤30s; non-target-scoped ezgha=managed label; missing --cap-drop ALL/egress/read-only rootfs; dash-prefixed image value defeats hardening flags; JIT credential in argv. Per rules these do NOT fail the evidence claims.
